title: QA manifesto yet another theory
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Q:A manifesto / yet another theory.             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 07, 2013 12:44 pm I really tried to find the fitting thread, but it covers so much ground, from fml, to my opinion of Q to some thoughts about the plot, that I have to share in this form. So I hope you forgive me. Q:A manifesto I’ve watched 3.0 twice while I was in Japan in December, which was quite the catharsis for me. As ridiculous as it may sound, Q is largely responsible for my sustained existence. I’ve gone through a pretty sinister phase in my life, and one major reason for me to move forward and not giving up was my decision to not leave until I have seen this movie. In the previous two years, every time, when I was on the verge of suicide I told to myself that I mustn’t kill myself, because I still had to see Q. I know that this sounds pretty borderline fanatic and almost religiously devoted, but I really felt that way. I had the feeling that I was all alone, so the idea of living for the sake of others didn’t really occur to me. Of course this simplifies the dilemma, and I don’t want to go too deeply into it. I just want to express, that the sheer existence of this movie, is very important to me. Q as an institution will always have a special place in my heart, apart from the movie and the story it tells. By the time the movie was finally released in theaters I was already recovering, and I had time and spared money, so I decided to fly to Tokyo. Not only for the movie, of course, since Tokyo has always been a place of fascination for me. So I booked the flight and one week later, I spend 20 days there. It was a marvelous experience brimmed with positive energy. On the first day of my trip I literally stumbled upon the Wald 9 cinema. It was already late, I was jetlegged, yet I decided to watch the movie. And what a joy it was. I think the movie was not able to disappoint me. The sheer fact of seeing the movie in Tokyo exhilarated me beyond reason. It felt like I could finally leave all the darkness behind me, as if seeing the movie would close this chapter in my life. Q might be the only object I am emotionally attached to such an extent. Now, to the movie itself. Q is interesting; it’s the slap in the face that I have been waiting for. For some, this slap might have hurt badly, but for me it was a good kind of pain. Now we’re into this mess together and can only try to analyze what exactly is going on. Or wait for the fourth movie, which could offer some explanations. But let’s be sincere; that would be so not Evangelion. I even think, that even some major points of confusion like Mari’s agenda won’t ever be addressed sufficiently, because there’s no reason to. But I’ll get to that later. In 3.0 lots of new stuff is introduced, a lot of build up from the previous movies is gone. One can only ask why Anno chose to do so, as an explanation is not given yet (man, I do really hope for a translation of CCR3.0). So I am asking: why did Anno choose to go this path? The obvious answer is, that they aimed for the biggest possible alienation not only for Shinji but also for the viewer. But wouldn’t a 2 year time skip have also done the trick? Misato would still have enough time to change her persona and solidify the hate and anger 90% of the cast has against Shinji. So, I started thinking; why whopping 14 years, especially that you have to consider how much this decision linked up with all it’s consequences strain on the suspension of disbelief? Then it occurred to me. When did we have the last huge time skip in the Evangelion franchise? Right! After the second impact. And after the second impact there was a gap of fifteen years, until other angels started attacking. After near-third impact: again fourteen years. So for whatever reason, after an impact there’s 14-15 years time until the angels start attacking again. That may be a little weak but for me it suffices to explain why between the 10th and 11th Angel there were those story-wise convenient fourteen years. Now to the real problem of the time skip: the non-aging pilots. Well, Shinji was trapped in Eva, so we have an explanation for that. Rei is a clone and Kaworu non-human. We don’t know how long Asuka was recovering in her coffin, it could be almost until the begin of Q, since it’s not stated otherwise. But then there’s Mari, and she's still looking young, although there’s no obvious reason for her to to so. So she’s kinda breaking the universe. (Which was her purpose anyway, like Anno stated.) Well, then I thought further. We don’t really know anything about the pasts of Asuka and Mari. And we still don’t know why some children are able to pilot Evas, while others aren’t (except for the feeble "it's fate" from Misato in 1.11). We only can assume that Shinji can, because his mother is in Eva 01, we never see him piloting another Units, and we don’t know if it’s possible for him. Except for Unit 13, but there’s also Kaworu in it, who is basically a vessel for Adam and an Angel and space Jesus, which circumvents this issue. As we have seen in 2.22, in rebuild, we do not have one but four Adams. And we have four children other than Shinji. We know from Asuka, Mari and Rei, that they are all capable of piloting other units than their native ones. Asuka piloted 2 and 4, Mari 2, 5 and 8, and Rei 1 and 9. This leads me to the assumption, that they do not need the souls of their respective mothers in the units to synchronize with them. So maybe they are all vessels of Adam like Kaworu is. This leads me back to my statement that we do not need to know the back-stories of all the other pilotes, because they are all like Kaworu; they do not really have one. We know, that Rei is a special case, and maybe so are Mari and Asuka, which we certainly will find out if this is the case. If this was true, this wouldn’t only be convenient for the movie, because it has a perfectly fine reason to be focused on Shinji (while forgetting everyone else), it would also explain the non-aging. You can go further; it would additionally shed the events of the near-third impact into a new light. Maybe it was not only Shinji’s will-power who set it in motion, but the fact that Rei was there, who is an Adam’s vessel. But this is highly speculative. This was quite a lot of text, but I needed to write it off my heart. I hope, maybe someone is interested in it, and I hope we can discuss this. Maybe I’ve overlooked something, which would make this idea completely obsolete, so I’m happy to hear your opinions.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Thu Feb 07, 2013 1:24 pm That's some ruff shit man, glad your still with us.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Feb 07, 2013 2:18 pm Interesting to see these movies have the same kind of inspiring effect the original series had on me when I was younger... Hang on, buddy, you're gonna make it. Life can't only bring good things and sooner or later, a bad thing is simply going to happen for statistical reasons, but the same is true the other way around: Sooner or later, a good thing has to happen. Our existence is already ridiculously short by cosmic standarts, no need to make it any shorter. Just find one thing to hang on to and focus on it (maybe proving those who put you in this situation wrong, or remembering the things you lost so it won't be like they never happened), other reasons to stay will come by by themselves. Or at least, that's how I once dealt with it, I don't know more than that I'm not an expert or anything and everyone has their own way... It was the same thing when our university recently showed the 2 first Rebuild movies during their university cinema program thingy (They say they're gonna try getting the licence for Q next semester) - I expected the University's asian population and a few nerds, but in the end, quite a lot people came and many of them were positively surprised, I heard some ppl talking how it was the best thing they had ever seen, and the majority of the people found the movies awesome and deep. I informed everyone of staying for the post-credit scene and that there's the original show and what the deal with the endings is, and many said they were gonna watch it. This being a German audience, they always snickered whenever there was a stab at the Americans or governments in general... and the laugh at Kaworu's final line was glorious. They all got the homoerotic vibes XD Not sure how they'll react to Q. To me, it threw me in a chrisis, it was the first time that a freakin TV show affected me in my "real life". I couldn't turn it off. Maybe it's different when you're already depressed? I remember when I was in the suckiest parts of my life, I sometimes DID feel like I had just broken my entire (personal) world by saying/doing something stupid and I couldn't see a way out, either. With the original series, Shinji did get an "It's all my fault"-complex towards the end, but we could tell its in his head. Maybe Anno is aiming at something / making us feel that pain much more by making all of it literary happen, with the very real in-universe situation still being a metaphor for a real-life mental downward spiral. Dunno, the original series was more of If it was a way to die, the original series plus EoE slowly strangles your little heart, Q flat out decapitates it. Or stuff. On some level, you can tell that Rebuild was written by someone with a girlfriend and the original by one without one. They're still very much about this feeling frustration and powerlessness, but that frustration is less sexual in nature, and while the original had the protagonist having... tension with like 4 ppl but never going very far with either (like when you're single and think every possible hottie you see could be a potential gf... or you wish she were, but there's probavly a reason you're single), Rebuild has him more or less focussed on Rei, and the frustration of losing her is less of the "You won't accept my advances" or "You won't give me sex!"-variety (see EoE), what I get from Q is more "I couldn't be the man you needed!"/"provide for you/protect you!", (because that is, essentially, what happened - one childish episode post Bardiel cost him everything.) he doesn't go fap, he smashes things. -- As for your theories, hm. I got the impression that EVAs/pilots already had some degree of interchangeability in the original. Rei talks like she could get into the EVAs 1 and 2 at any moment (ep 10 etc), Shinji could operate unit 2 to so degree, even if the results were worse than when Asuka helped. I gathered that the pilot's own mother simply works better . The CR says that the reason for Rei's lower synch ratio is that "Unit zero is a sucky prototype, but also, she doesn't have a mom that could be in there.". There's also that bit in episode 14 where they tried synching Shinji with Unit zero - It appeared to work at first, but the ratio was notably lower, probably lacking the bonus from having Yui in EVA 01. Then, of course, whatever was in EVA 00 decided to have him for breakfast... It was stated that what caused the explosion was the awakening, the entire red lights laser lighshow part. ("This is a device to circumvent awakenings." "Once awakened, EVA 01 opened the doors of guf and shit happened etc") The impact vortex appeared while he was messing with the core, long before Rei herself actually came anywhere near actually entering EVA 01, though it may or may not have been a requirement to get her out. My personal theory (admittedly just speculation) is that Shinji was mantaining an idea of himself through mostly force of will after he tanged (hence the blurry, flame-like outline of his "blue" form) and the moment he was all relieved to have Rei back and was mostly focussed on her, he stopped being focussed on controlling the EVA, and no longer exerting the extreme force of will needed to mantain a clear, linear conciousness in a tanged state (Even in the original, he needed three days to get to "Hey, where am I? What happened?", and if anything, retaining conciousness while tanged is harder in Rebuild, or maybe you just don't remember any of it? There IS the "Do something, baka" incident to consider, and the fact that it ended by itself. There was no stopping EVA 13, even if he was aware of what was happening that time - but then again, spears were involved, and his control of EVA 01 is probably better because Yui. 4I had a much more "chemical-reaction-like" progression, ... Was there any indication that he remembered anything in the one month he spent tanged in the original?), and thus lost control/ stopped being a factor in the equation, and there was still this EVA on the way of returning to its original form (which is a SoL copy - something that does impacts. SoLs are big white humanoid things, awakened EVAs are big white humanoid things. Coincidence? Dun think so. ) with no one left to control it. BTW, the gap wasn't between the 10th and 11th angel - It's assumed that the angels kept arriving at a regular shedule. The 12th is sealed in terminad dogma, and Kaworu said no one came down there for 14 years. So, angels arrived soon after TI, ppl probably had a hard time fighting them with all their infrastructure wrecked, something happened to produce that sorry sight in terminal Dogma, and then it got sealed. Perhaps it was the 12th angel's sealing that "halted the clock", as the next and the instrumentality of the one after could only happen after it's done. Hence why Gendo ordering Rei to free the 12th leads to a 13th angel manifestation. Or something like that, in any case, liberating angel nr 12 seems to be an exclusively Gendonian addition to the plan, since he had Rei do it. Mari's and his own comments suggest that at least the Kaworu situation was his handiwork, even if everything else was arranged by SEELE. Probably including having the spears switched, since they were the mayor key thing, and everything went almost according to their plan, apparently.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 07, 2013 3:21 pm hui43210 wrote: That's some ruff shit man, glad your still with us.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Thu Feb 07, 2013 4:12 pm "Gendonian"? I hope that ends up in the Oxford Dictionary one day. Just as planned, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Feb 07, 2013 4:30 pm robersora wrote: May I ask, which University that would be?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 07, 2013 5:09 pm Kendrix wrote: The Saarland University in Germany.

[]:             Quote Post by Alpha » Thu Feb 07, 2013 6:00 pm robersora wrote: Do we know Kaworu's rebuldian surname?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 07, 2013 6:21 pm Alpha wrote: Still Nagisa, judging by Shinji calling him "Nagisa-kun" so often.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Feb 08, 2013 2:48 am robersora wrote: Of course it isn't, but in rebuild we never see Shinji piloting other EVAs, not even for tests. Is he even considered to be the pilot for Unit 03?

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Fri Feb 08, 2013 6:16 am Kendrix wrote: T She says that the laser-incident was one of the reasons for putting a bomb on him, .

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 08, 2013 6:32 am Kendrix wrote: Kaworu explicitly states a few times that it takes two different souls to use both spears, and that Rei was unusable for reasons related to her being artificial - presumably some fail-safe by the FAR to prevent "forbidden fusions"/ combination of both types of spears, so no one gets whatever crazy dangerous power you'd get with two spears, and Gendo tried to have that circumvented by putting two soulsin one EVA. - there does not seem anything preventig a single person from grabbing two spears of the same type, other than, of course, the risk of causing an impact if sufficient quantities of angelic matter are present... Then again, the whole deal may have been a lie of SEELE's - Mari implies that the real reason for putting them both in there is to have a backup impact trigger if one of them should die. If one goes splat, the other's still in there, and in a heightened emotional state, to boot.

Article: QA manifesto yet another theory
 that it takes two different souls to use both spears, and that Rei was unusable for reasons related to her being artificial - presumably some fail-safe by the FAR to prevent "forbidden fusions"/ combination of both types of spears, so no one gets whatever crazy dangerous power you'd get with two spears, and Gendo tried to have that circumvented by putting two soulsin one EVA. - there does not seem anything preventig a single person from grabbing two spears of the same type, other than, of course, the risk of causing an impact if sufficient quantities of angelic matter are present... Then again, the whole deal may have been a lie of SEELE's - Mari implies that the real reason for putting them both in there is to have a backup impact trigger if one of them should die. If one goes splat, the other's still in there, and in a heightened emotional state, to boot.
